summary: R2 fixes the prior P1 accessibility-label finding for the explicitly named strings: virtualized district rows, map-mode button accessible names, sort button accessible names, map keyboard help, selected-district live status, map role description, map renderer failure text, and developer-workspace label now route through packages/ui/src/i18n.ts, with zh-CN/system fallback positive and English-negative coverage. However, independent review found a remaining default player-mode visible English string: the active sort button renders aria-hidden but visually visible text suffixes " up" and " down" in packages/ui/src/index.tsx. This still violates the task acceptance criterion that all player-visible app-shell strings use the accepted i18n foundation, and current zh-CN/system fallback tests pass without catching it.

decisions.decision: The R2 implementation resolves the prior hard-coded accessibility-label set and adds zh-CN/system fallback assertions that fail if those English accessible names return.
Acceptance is still blocked because visible default player-mode sort direction text uses English up/down literals instead of the accepted i18n foundation.
No security_reviewer escalation is required from this QA pass; R2 touched UI/i18n/test files only, and no Electron IPC or platform security surface changed.

risks.summary: Current zh-CN/system fallback tests cover accessible names but not visible active-sort direction text, so they pass while a default player-mode English suffix remains visible.

requested_action: Localize the active sort direction indicator in packages/ui/src/index.tsx instead of rendering literal English " up" / " down". Either use localized i18n keys for ascending/descending indicators or replace the visible suffix with locale-neutral iconography while preserving accessible sort state. Add a focused zh-CN/system fallback regression that would fail if the rendered active sort button contains English up/down in default player mode.
